risk_levels.low: Narrow scope and safe for most engineering tasks.
risk_levels.medium: Broader external access or automation that should be enabled intentionally.
risk_levels.high: Expands the trust boundary through localhost access, broad filesystem reach, or remote package execution.

servers.description: GitHub repository, PR, issue, and code search workflows.
servers.notes: Default baseline for repository context. Scope is limited to connected GitHub data.

servers.description: Primary-source library and framework documentation retrieval.
servers.notes: Useful for official technical docs without broad browsing.

servers.description: Structured local memory graph for reusable project observations.
servers.notes: Local project memory only. Keep data hygiene in mind when storing observations.

servers.description: Structured reasoning helper for complex planning and review tasks.
servers.notes: No external side effects. Safe baseline analysis support.

servers.description: External neural web and code search for current information.
servers.notes: External browsing and search should be explicitly enabled.

servers.description: Browser automation, snapshots, screenshots, and UI flow inspection.
servers.notes: Automates a real browser. Useful, but not part of the default baseline.

servers.description: Security-focused static analysis for common application vulnerabilities.
servers.notes: Security scanner with optional cloud-backed rules. Enable only for review flows that need it.

servers.description: Dependency vulnerability lookups against the OSV database.
servers.notes: Useful for supply-chain review. External lookup, but read-only.

servers.description: Broad local filesystem access beyond the current workspace.
servers.notes: Broad host access materially expands the trust boundary and must stay opt-in.

servers.description: Bridge to developer-run localhost services and private APIs.
servers.notes: Placeholder for localhost integrations. Exact endpoint must be user-supplied at install time.

servers.description: MCP servers executed through ad hoc package manager download flows.
servers.notes: Remote package execution is cataloged for transparency but prohibited from safe defaults.
